sections.paragraph:
τρυπάω (s. τρῦπα) fut. τρυπήσω; 1 aor. ἐτρύπησα; pf. pass. ptc. masc. acc. τετρυπημένον Hg 1:6 (Hom. et al.; Hero Alex. I p. 4, 23; 36, 14; LXX; GrBar) make a hole in, bore through τί someth. τὸν οὐρανόν the sky Hm 11:18 (GrBar 3:7). τὸν λίθον 11:20.—B. 593. DELG.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

masc. masc. = masculine

acc. acc. = accusative

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

someth. someth. = something

Hm Hm = Mandates

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1018
